last_item: Roles & Databases — the one-time SecretsModal ('Save these now', shown after New App / rotate) shows passwords + connection strings that 'cannot be retrieved again', but was a plain Modal, so Escape, a backdrop click, OR the corner X each routed to onClose -> setSecrets(null), destroying the only copy before the user copied it (recovery = rotate again). A reflexive dismiss = irreversible credential loss. Surfaced by the iter-19 Roles/Backups convergence agent; same 'harden an irreversible path' class as iter-18's rotate-confirm fix. Verified against the code (Modal.tsx routes Escape/backdrop/X via Radix onOpenChange; dialog.tsx renders the X by default with showCloseButton=true).
last_result: SHIPPED (4 SHIP, zero blockers — incl. restraint critic). Subtractive fix: added an opt-in dismissible?: boolean prop to the shared Modal (default true -> every other modal byte-for-byte unchanged). When false it sets showCloseButton={false} (drops the corner X) and preventDefaults onEscapeKeyDown + onInteractOutside (swallows Escape + backdrop/focus-out) — all three Radix dismiss vectors closed in concert. SecretsModal now passes dismissible={false}, making the existing 'I've saved them' footer button (always rendered, so never trapped) the sole exit. Net surface REMOVES the X, adds no control/copy/click. Verified SecretValue Reveal/Copy are real keyboard-reachable Buttons inside the focus trap (heuristics reviewer's one non-blocking note — already satisfied). Extended the rotate test: after confirm, the secrets dialog has no Close (X) button, an Escape keydown does NOT dismiss it, and only 'I've saved them' closes it. Panel: UX heuristics ('textbook H#5 error-prevention; all three dismiss vectors closed together; works by subtraction'); Sam ('the safe direction to fail in; footer button always renders so I'm never trapped'); Priya ('the one place a reflexive dismiss is irreversible loss; scoped to SecretsModal, every other dialog keeps Escape/click-away; guardrail not hand-holding'); restraint critic ('removes a control, adds none; dismissible is a safe-default opt-in with a concrete caller, NOT premature config; a bespoke SecretsModal would be MORE divergence; do-nothing accepts guaranteed credential loss'). Gates: typecheck OK, 145 tests OK (extended existing rotate test, not a new case), build OK (dist regenerated + staged), go build OK (exit 0, outside sandbox). stable_streak stays 0 (shipped a real improvement). LESSON: once-only content must close only by an explicit acknowledgement — strip ALL casual dismiss paths (X + Escape + backdrop) rather than adding a warning; a safe-default opt-in dismissible boolean with one concrete caller clears restraint because it's subtractive and the loss it prevents is irreversible. Backlog is now actionable-empty again (NEEDS-BACKEND item + two low/watch items). Next iteration: run a fresh Mode-S discovery/convergence pass.
notes: PURE UX-IMPROVEMENT LOOP. Sibling of the COMPLETED scripts/ralph/ shadcn-migration track. Capability parity is sacred (Vitest suite is the contract); layout/flow/copy are free to change. Restraint over churn -- rejecting a change and recording why is a normal outcome. Frontend/UX only, never touch Go. go build is blocked in-sandbox (snap-confine); run with sandbox disabled. See PROMPT.md + UX-PRINCIPLES.md + PERSONAS.md + UI-RULES.md.
north_star: The indiepg panel is the easiest, clearest, safest way for an indie hacker to run their Postgres -- minimal surface, co-located workflows, honest state, no over-design. Stop when the UX has converged (stable_streak >= 3).
